title: Campimia scorpioides
intro: Campimia scorpioides, also known as Kalmegh or Small Leaf Kalmegh, is a perennial plant native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Apiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which may help to manage conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Campimia scorpioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Campimia scorpioides is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or juice, and is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Campimia scorpioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is a perennial herb that grows up to 30-60 cm tall. Campimia scorpioides is also known as Scorpion's Tail or Campion. Its classification makes it a part of the larger Campanula genus.
